summary: Feature request to publish static library (.lib/.a) NuGet packages for SkiaSharp to enable NativeAOT single-file publishing on desktop and server platforms.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: NativeAOT / single-file publish scenarios; no specific platform mentioned

repoLinks.description: Avalonia issue requesting NativeAOT / static lib support
NativeAOT P/Invoke interop and static linking documentation
Sentry example of MSBuild .targets for auto-linking native libs in AOT mode

analysis.summary: The reporter wants to produce a single NativeAOT executable that statically links SkiaSharp's native library. Currently SkiaSharp ships only shared/dynamic libraries (.so, .dylib, .dll) in its NativeAssets packages. NativeAOT requires static libraries (.lib on Windows, .a on Unix) to link native code into the final binary at build time. The WASM target already uses static linking via Emscripten .a files and NativeFileReference, serving as a precedent. This request is valid and non-trivial — it would require building and distributing platform-specific static libraries.
analysis.rationale: The issue is clearly a feature request: it describes a desired new distribution format (static libs) not currently provided by SkiaSharp. The WASM precedent shows the team is capable of shipping static assets, but desktop/server static libs require a different build configuration in the Skia build system. The 27 upvotes and the recent comment referencing Sentry's MSBuild targets confirm there is real demand. No duplicate issue found.

codeInvestigation.finding: WASM NativeAssets already ship .a static libraries linked via Emscripten NativeFileReference — confirms static lib distribution is feasible for one target. Desktop targets only ship dynamic libraries.
Template NativeAssets nuspec only references tools/ with no static lib patterns, confirming no static libs are produced or packaged currently.
Publishing modes table shows Single-file mode extracts native binaries alongside the executable at runtime — this is not compatible with NativeAOT's requirement for compile-time static linking.

keySignals.text: I want to build single NativeAOT file with SkiaSharp related projects like Avalonia.
keySignals.interpretation: Core use case: NativeAOT single-file publish, which requires static linking of native code.

keySignals.text: Please publish static lib in your nuget packages.
keySignals.interpretation: Clear, actionable request: ship .lib/.a files in NativeAssets packages.

keySignals.text: Why a backlog? This is important for publishing single app applications.
keySignals.interpretation: Community pressure; the feature is valued beyond the original reporter.

keySignals.text: here is an example of how the guys from sentry made automatic linking of applications in AOT mode
keySignals.interpretation: A concrete implementation path exists via MSBuild .targets that auto-wires static libs.

workarounds: For WASM targets NativeAOT-style static linking already works via SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly.

For desktop NativeAOT: use the dynamic library with P/Invoke by setting PublishAot=true — NativeAOT can still P/Invoke shared libraries when deploying them alongside the executable, though this does not produce a true single-file binary.

resolution.hypothesis: Static library packages would need to be built with the Skia build system in static mode for each target platform (Windows x64/arm64, Linux x64/arm64, macOS universal) and distributed as new NuGet packages (e.g., SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32.Static). An MSBuild .targets file would wire them as NativeFileReference items for NativeAOT toolchain.

proposals.title: Build and publish static library NativeAssets packages
proposals.description: Extend the Skia build scripts to produce static libraries (.lib/.a) in addition to dynamic ones. Create new NuGet packages (e.g., SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32.Static) containing the .lib/.a files and an MSBuild .targets that adds them as NativeFileReference for NativeAOT. This approach mirrors how WASM static assets are shipped.

proposals.title: Ship static libs inside existing NativeAssets packages
proposals.description: Add static library artifacts to the existing NativeAssets packages alongside the dynamic libraries, guarded by a Condition on PublishAot=true. Less discoverable but avoids a separate package.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Build and publish static library NativeAssets packages
resolution.recommendedReason: Separate packages maintain clean separation of concerns, avoids bloating existing packages for projects that don't use NativeAOT, and follows the WASM precedent.

actionability.reason: Valid and well-supported feature request with 27 upvotes; no implementation exists yet and it requires significant build system work. Should stay open as a tracked enhancement.

actions.description: Apply feature-request, native lib area, and compatibility tenet labels
Acknowledge request, explain current status, describe what implementation would require
actions.comment:
Thanks for the request! Shipping static library packages for NativeAOT is a valid and interesting scenario.

Right now SkiaSharp's NativeAssets packages only contain dynamic libraries (`.so`, `.dylib`, `.dll`). The WASM target already ships static `.a` files for Emscripten linking, which shows the pattern is feasible.

For desktop NativeAOT, implementing this would require:
1. Building Skia in static library mode for each target platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) — non-trivial, as the Skia build system needs specific flags and all transitive native deps must also be static or absorbed.
2. Creating new NuGet packages (e.g., `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32.Static`) with the `.lib`/`.a` files and an MSBuild `.targets` that wires them as `NativeFileReference`.
3. The Sentry `.targets` example linked in the comments is a useful reference for the MSBuild integration piece.

In the meantime, a partial workaround is to use NativeAOT with the dynamic library — NativeAOT can still P/Invoke shared libraries deployed alongside the executable, though this doesn't produce a true single-file binary.
